SHA256 update loop with bulk uint32 byte conversion.
Previously, each page hash was computed by looping over tokens in Python:
for t in token_ids:
    hasher.update(t.to_bytes(4, byteorder="little", sig

sglang.title: feat(hicache): Use NIXL path-mode

Motivation
The worker process spends a lot of time holding the GIL, thus every file.open() called from python introduces a delay because of the GIL lock re-acquire. NIXL can now use the path-mode ( ai-dynamo/nixl#1635 , available since NIXL release 1.3.0 ), which batch-opens the files in the native

sglang.title: feat(mem_cache): unified memory pool for hybrid Mamba / SWA models
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Hybrid Mamba/GDN and hybrid SWA models maintain two caches: the full-attention KV cache and the per-request Mamba conv/SSM (or SWA) state. Today each lives in a separately-sized pool, so the capacity split between them is fixed at startup — one can run dry while the other has slack.
This

sglang.title: [chore] Add no-getattr rule; refine no-dataclasses rule
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
AI agents (and defensive-programming habits generally) tend to reach for getattr / hasattr on objects whose fields are always present. This hides real errors and defeats strict type checking. For example:
revision=getattr(server_args, "revision", None),   # server_args ALWAYS has revision

sglang.title: [AMD]: docker(rocm) bump Mooncake to latest main + enable multi-protocol
sglang.author: Lzy17
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The old Mooncake pin lacks the multi-protocol/hip_transport machinery: with USE_HIP=ON the engine became XGMI-only and broke RDMA with NotSupportedTransport. Latest main plus ENABLE_MULTI_PROTOCOL lets RDMA (cross-node) and HIP/XGMI (intra-node) coexist with runtime transport selection. A

sglang.title: fix(precision): do not promote failed runs to the comparison baseline
sglang.author: JustinTong0323
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Follow-up to #26902 (nightly precision regression test).
Problem
_select_latest_run selects the comparison baseline purely by recency (push_index) and ignores pass_label. The FAILED branch of the nightly test uploads the regressed tensors to the HF store with pass_label="failed" (intended only to pr

sglang.title: Fix MambaPool.clear_slots OOM by replacing expand-based tensor allocation with scalar zeroing

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
NPU OOM was observed when launching the Qwen3.6-35B-A3B service. The root cause is in MambaPool.clear_slots, which creates temporary expanded tensors via torch.zeros(1).expand(...) for clearing mamba pool slots. On NPU, this allocates significant temporary memory during each extend forwar

Extracts the per-request conv-state plumbing shared by hybrid short-conv models into one model-agnostic attention backend, and migrates ZAYA1 (CCA) and LFM2 / LFM2-MoE onto it. The models no longer touch the pool directly — they receive a ShortConvMetadata handle and run their own conv kerne

customized_info is later indexed by output-request position, so each key must

sglang.title: Fix capture-mode detection during breakable CUDA graph capture

Problem
Prefill's breakable CUDA graph (BCG) captured a max_num_tokens (e.g. 4096) EP all-to-all buffer (~2GB) instead of the actual x.shape[0] (~512).
Cause
Prefill BCG capture doesn't enter model_capture_mode(), so get_is_capture_mode() was False during BCG capture. The FlashInfer a2a dispatcher's

vllm.bodyExcerpt: Removed ut qk_norm_rope_fusion cuda hard code, added xpu support.

vllm.title: Weight sync refactor + move sparse nccl engine
vllm.author: hao-aaron
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Current state of the weight sync path requires non weight transfer engine components to assume too much about weight sync internals. Gpu workers should not need to worry about whether to run layerwise reload, or how to construct weight loaders for weight transfer engines. These concepts are

vllm.title: [ROCm][DSV4] Use aiter mHC pre/post as the default ROCm path
vllm.author: Fangzhou-Ai
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Re-enable the aiter multi-head-consensus (mHC) pre/post ops as the preferred ROCm path for DeepSeek V4. #43679 introduced the tilelang fused post+pre mHC kernel and explicitly left a hook to switch back to the (faster) aiter mHC kernels once an aiter release containing the mhc_pre_gemm_sqrsu

vllm.title: [Test] Run SageMaker handler-override tests in-process via TestClient
vllm.author: Jyothirmaikottu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The SageMaker handler-override integration tests in
tests/entrypoints/serve/sagemaker/test_sagemaker_handler_overrides.py each
launched a full model server via RemoteOpenAIServer, only to exercise
customer handlers that fully replace the /ping and /invocations
endpoints. No inference engine

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix pooled Whisper sliding-window KV sizing
vllm.author: andylolu2
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Voxtral Realtime's causal Whisper encoder uses block_pool_size encoder tokens per pooled KV block, but its SlidingWindowSpec.sliding_window was still expressed in encoder-token units. The sliding-window KV cache manager then reserved about block_pool_size times too many blocks for the encode

vllm.title: [ROCm][Perf][Bugfix] DSv4 indexer: use platform FP8 dtype (fnuz) for Q-quant on gfx942
vllm.author: akii96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
On gfx942 the DeepSeek-V4 Flash indexer quantizes Q and K to different FP8 types. K already uses the platform type (e4m3fnuz on gfx942, via current_platform.fp8_dtype()), but the fused RoPE+quant kernel in fused_indexer_q.py hardcodes Q to e4m3fn. The FP8 logits kernel then gets fnuz K wi

vllm.title: [CI] Fix various failures on `main`
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
#43586 missed Gemma3ForConditionalGeneration when adding path to the interface for various methods (Multi-Modal Models (Extended Generation 1))
#47000 used torch.accelerator.set_device which doesn't exist (Distributed Tests (2xH100-2xMI300))
#46635 added Tensor.split calls which produced non-contigu

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Expose usage field in GenerateResponse for disaggregated serving
vllm.author: AIvashov
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
serving.py already constructs UsageInfo (including prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens when --enable-prompt-tokens-details is set) and passes it to the GenerateResponse constructor. However, the usage field was missing from the GenerateResponse model, so pydantic silently dropped it.
Fix: ad

vllm.title: [Model] Support Hy3 token suffix and JSON Schema array types
vllm.author: stevenkuang-tencent
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Support Hy3 token suffix and JSON Schema array types.
Test Plan
Tests for the hy_v3 reasoning parser and tool parser.​
Test Result
Passed


 Add two enhancements to the hy_v3 reasoning parser and tool parser 

Hy3 tokenizers append a suffix to the special tokens. Read the suffix from tokeniz

vllm.title: [Model] Remove AyaVision, MusicFlamingo
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Reasons:

Both of these architecture classes recieved very low vLLM usage
MusicFlamingo has been superseded by AudioFlamingo3 (new MusicFlamingo checkpoints use AudioFlamingo3)

This PR removes both architecture classes and adds them to _PREVIOUSLY_SUPPORTED_MODELS

vllm.title: [ROCm][MiniMax-M3] Cross-layer lightning-indexer top-k sharing
vllm.author: Fangzhou-Ai
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Adds opt-in cross-layer sharing of the lightning-indexer top-k block selection for MiniMax-M3 sparse attention. When use_index_cache is set, only 1 of every index_topk_freq sparse-attention layers recomputes the indexer score + top-k selection; the rest reuse the selection the preceding comp

vllm.title: docs(security): document gRPC interface as insecure for private use only
vllm.author: jperezdealgaba
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Add a new section to the security guide documenting that the gRPC interface does not implement authentication, authorization, or encryption and must be protected via network-level access controls.

vllm.title: Migrate GPTBigCode and Starcoder2 to the Transformers modeling backend
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Migrate support of GPTBigCode and Starcoder2 to use the Transformers modeling backend instead of having duplicated implementations in vLLM.
These architectures are ~2 years old and do not feature in the top 100 most used architectures in vLLM.
This migration:

Reduces the number of model implementat

vllm.title: [CI] Fix segfault in tracing test

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This was actually already fixed for rocm case but it is not rocm-specific and the regular metrics test fails intermittently due to this.
E.g. https://buildkite.com/vllm/ci/builds/75740/list?jid=019f1d4f-1e55-4e15-b6db-fd8f495f8bef&tab=output

vllm.author: chaojun-zhang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
This PR fixes several bugs after #42290 and #41184 .
Fixes
1. Rank dimension mismatch in _apply_expand for TP > 1 (non-fully-sharded LoRA)
2. Hardcoded add_inputs=True in add_lora_linear
The add_inputs kwarg was popped but add_expand was always called with add_inputs=True, ignoring the calle

vllm.author: BugenZhao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Signed-off-by: Bugen Zhao i@bugenzhao.com
Purpose
Pure code structure refactoring without any logic changes, making the engine-core-client crate cleaner than mixing all definitions into mod.rs. Now we have:

request
output
sampling
structured_outputs

This also helps if we want to isolate the northb

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix GraniteMoeShared weight loading broken by #41184
vllm.author: mganczarenko
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix KeyError when loading GraniteMoeSharedForCausalLM weights after the
FusedMoE/MoERunner inversion refactor (#41184).
After #41184, the parent _load_weights() expects expert keys of the form
experts.{e}.w{n}.weight. GraniteMoeShared.load_weights() was still
inserting routed_experts before

vllm.title: [Spec Decode] DSpark
vllm.author: benchislett
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Adds support for DSpark speculative decoding, using both DeepSeek-V4 DSpark models as well as DeepSeek-trained Qwen3-DSpark models.
Design
DSpark uses non-causal sliding-window attention. To implement this, instead of manually reimplementing the MLA attention, we instead utilize the existing

vllm.title: [Bench][BugFix] Fix empty decoder prompt for Cohere ASR in throughput benchmark
vllm.author: mganczarenko
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix ASRDataset.sample() raising "The decoder prompt cannot be empty" when
benchmarking Cohere ASR models (e.g. CohereLabs/cohere-transcribe-03-2026).
ASRDataset.sample() previously only built a decoder prompt for OpenAI/Whisper
tokenizers, falling back to prompt = "" for all others. CohereAs

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Prevent padding placeholders from reaching embeddings
vllm.author: qianlihuang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Fix a CUDA device-side assert that can occur when speculative padding placeholders are scheduled as draft tokens.
Scheduler-side speculative padding uses -1 draft token IDs to keep the first decode step at the uniform 1 + num_spec_tokens shape. Those placeholder IDs should be visible to reje

vllm.title: [ROCm][P/D] MoRIIO toy proxy: support JSON Content-Type for OpenAI clients.
vllm.author: lcskrishna
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The Quart-based MoRIIO disaggregated toy proxy returns streamed responses with Quart's defaults via make_response(stream_generator) without setting a Content-Type.
Quart defaults a streamed response to text/html; charset=utf-8, so even on a successful 200 the body is mislabeled.
Reference Do

vllm.title: [ModelRunner V2] Warmup cross-attn properly in encoder-decoder case
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: E.g. for whisper. Existing warmup was resulting in zero-key attention op.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Don't read KV cache past `seq_len` in triton paged attn kernels
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The Triton unified_attention and ROCm paged-decode kernels load KV cache slots beyond seq_len (the unwritten tail of the last partial block) and rely on the score mask to drop them. That is unsafe when the tail is non-finite: masked positions get softmax weight 0, but 0 * NaN = NaN still poisons the

vllm.title: [DSV4] Better MXFP8 quantization kernel
vllm.author: zyongye
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Apparently this cutedsl kernel is 2x of the original cuda kernel

vllm.title: [MoE] Plumb gemm1_alpha/beta/clamp_limit into TRT-LLM FP8 MoE
vllm.author: zyongye
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The FlashInfer FP8 block-scale MoE kernels — trtllm_fp8_block_scale_moe and trtllm_fp8_block_scale_routed_moe — accept three optional per-expert SwiGLU parameters (gemm1_alpha, gemm1_beta, gemm1_clamp_limit) that realize the OAI SwiGLU variant (X2 * sigmoid(alpha*X2) * (X1 + beta) with clamp

vllm.title: [CI] Remove torch_nightly mirror tags (superseded by TORCH_NIGHTLY full-nightly build)
vllm.author: atalman
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Remove the torch_nightly step tags that drive the curated "vLLM Against PyTorch Nightly" mirror group.
This mirror mechanism selects a subset of steps (tagged torch_nightly: true / mirror: { torch_nightly: {} }) that the ci-infra pipeline generator collects into a separate "vLLM Against PyTo

vllm.author: atalman
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Remove docker/Dockerfile.nightly_torch, which is dead/unreferenced.
Why it's safe
Dockerfile.nightly_torch (added in #16936) is not referenced anywhere:

vLLM repo: no hits in .buildkite/ pipelines, docker/*.hcl bake files, image_build*.sh scripts, .github/workflows, or docs.
vllm-project/ci

vllm.title: [UX] Include NVTX in cuda.txt
vllm.author: jeejeelee
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Our code uses nvtx, so add it to cuda.txt, otherwise, when we set export VLLM_NVTX_SCOPES_FOR_PROFILING=1, the server will raise the following error.

(EngineCore pid=882710) ERROR 07-01 17:42:14 [core.py:1233]          ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

vllm.title: [BugFix][Spec Decode] Compact shared topk indices buffer after first MTP draft step
vllm.author: TheEpicDolphin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Context
Deepseek-style topk index selection for MTP has a bug during proposal stage when the topk indices are shared among all MTP draft step forward passes. After the first draft step, set_skip_topk is called on the MTP model to force the remaining draft steps to reuse the same topk indices values

vllm.title: [ROCm][MLA] Fuse MLA q/kv RMSNorm + FP8 per-token quant in the FP8 attention path
vllm.author: xaguilar-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
The MLADualRMSNormFusionPass fuses the paired q_a_layernorm +
kv_a_layernorm of MLA attention into AITER's fused_qk_rmsnorm HIP kernel, but
that fusion silently stops firing once attention is FP8-quantized — the
earlier RocmAiterRMSNormQuantFusionPass folds the q-side
rms_norm → fp8 per-toke

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Kernel] Correct FlashInfer CUTLASS MoE tuning token bound
vllm.author: Aneureka
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Previously, #34542 refactored the FlashInfer CUTLASS fused MoE backend and passed the CUDA graph max capture size (typically 512) as tune_max_num_tokens for flashinfer_cutlass_fused_moe. However, max_capture_size describes graph capture capacity and can be unrelated to the scheduler/MoE max
